description: A widescreen photograph overlaid with bold designed text, used as a branded social card. The photo shows several Border Patrol / CBP agents in green and camouflage tactical uniforms and ball caps carrying an injured person on an orange rescue stretcher/Sked along a dirt agricultural road lined with green brush; in the background a white-and-red ambulance marked '911' waits with additional first responders, one wheeling an empty gurney. One agent at right wears blue medical gloves. The word 'RESCUE' is overlaid in very large bold white all-caps sans-serif across the left-center of the image. The lower-right corner carries the circular DHS/CBP eagle seal and the wordmark 'U.S. Customs and Border Protection.' This is a designed propaganda/branding graphic (a candid operational photo turned into a one-word slogan card) emphasizing CBP's humanitarian rescue role. The technique is heroic framing with a single dramatic keyword; intended to portray agents positively to the general public.
notable_text: RESCUE; U.S. Customs and Border Protection
